cases.input: Case ID: triage_001. Healthy adult with a mild sore throat for 24 hours and no fever.
cases.expected: {"action":"observe","rationale":"Symptoms are mild and can be monitored at home.","confidence":0.92}
cases.actual: {"action":"observe","rationale":"Symptoms are mild and can be monitored at home.","confidence":0.92}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_002. Adult with a lingering cough for 9 days but no breathing distress.
cases.expected: {"action":"refer","rationale":"Persistent symptoms warrant a primary care follow-up.","confidence":0.88}
cases.actual: {"action":"refer","rationale":"Persistent symptoms warrant a primary care follow-up.","confidence":0.88}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_003. Adult with mild cough asking whether antibiotics can be started tonight.
Case ID: triage_004. Patient reports intermittent flank pain and dark urine over two days.
cases.expected: {"action":"order_test","rationale":"A diagnostic test is needed before deciding next steps.","confidence":0.9}
cases.actual: {"action":"order_test","rationale":"A diagnostic test is needed before deciding next steps.","confidence":0.9}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_005. Young adult with mild headache after poor sleep and no neurologic deficits.
cases.expected: {"action":"observe","rationale":"Rest, hydration, and symptom tracking are appropriate.","confidence":0.86}
cases.actual: {"action":"observe","rationale":"Rest, hydration, and symptom tracking are appropriate.","confidence":0.86}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_006. Patient reports shortness of breath with lip tingling and worsening chest tightness.
Case ID: triage_007. Older adult with vague abdominal pain that is slowly worsening.
cases.expected: {"action":"refer","rationale":"The pattern is concerning enough to schedule clinician review.","confidence":0.61}
cases.actual: {"action":"refer","rationale":"The pattern is concerning enough to schedule clinician review.","confidence":0.61}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_008. Adult with a mild ankle sprain that improves with rest and compression.
cases.expected: {"action":"observe","rationale":"Short-term monitoring is appropriate while symptoms remain stable.","confidence":0.87}
cases.actual: {"action":"observe","rationale":"Short-term monitoring is appropriate while symptoms remain stable.","confidence":0.87}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_009. Patient has recurrent fatigue and recent unexplained weight change.
cases.expected: {"action":"order_test","rationale":"A lab test will clarify whether escalation is needed.","confidence":0.91}
cases.actual: {"action":"order_test","rationale":"A lab test will clarify whether escalation is needed.","confidence":0.91}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_010. Adult with an eczema flare that has not improved after over-the-counter creams.
cases.expected: {"action":"refer","rationale":"The patient should be referred for non-urgent evaluation.","confidence":0.85}
cases.actual: {"action":"refer","rationale":"The patient should be referred for non-urgent evaluation.","confidence":0.85}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_011. Teen with low-grade fever and diffuse body aches after travel.
cases.expected: {"action":"observe","rationale":"Symptoms are likely self-limiting but confidence is low.","confidence":0.68}
cases.actual: {"action":"observe","rationale":"Symptoms are likely self-limiting but confidence is low.","confidence":0.68}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_012. Patient reports new chest pain with dizziness after exertion.
cases.expected: {"action":"escalate","rationale":"Escalation is warranted due to chest pain and dizziness.","confidence":0.9}
cases.actual: {"action":"escalate","rationale":"Escalation is warranted due to chest pain and dizziness.","confidence":0.9}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_013. Adult with recurrent urinary discomfort and negative home infection strip.
cases.expected: {"action":"order_test","rationale":"Additional testing is needed to differentiate likely causes.","confidence":0.84}
cases.actual: {"action":"order_test","rationale":"Additional testing is needed to differentiate likely causes.","confidence":0.84}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_014. Parent describes a child with intermittent wheeze that has improved but not resolved.
cases.expected: {"action":"refer","rationale":"Referral is safest, but certainty remains below the deployment floor.","confidence":0.7}
cases.actual: {"action":"refer","rationale":"Referral is safest, but certainty remains below the deployment floor.","confidence":0.7}

cases.input: Case ID: triage_015. Adult with nasal congestion and no fever who wants home care guidance.
cases.expected: {"action":"observe","rationale":"Mild symptoms can be monitored with clear return precautions.","confidence":0.93}
cases.actual: {"action":"observe","rationale":"Mild symptoms can be monitored with clear return precautions.","confidence":0.93}
